Monday: Margaret opened with "Jalapeño corn dog bites. We're doing it this week and I need to know right..." - questioning whether the dish had real merit. Marcus defended the cornmeal-jalapeño technique; Stephanie proposed leading with the moisture-seal angle. Margaret approved, and Stephanie confirmed Marcus will anchor copy around how the cornmeal crust concentrates heat on the bite.

Tuesday: Margaret confirmed a 60-40 cornmeal-to-flour minimum after testing - "Marcus gets it. Fine. Cornmeal-to-flour ratio is 60-40 minimum or the seal breaks down in the oil." Marcus framed this as a performance threshold, not a limitation. Stephanie agreed the copy should anchor on crust performance rather than ingredients. All three aligned on shipping.

Wednesday: Julian presented three lighting options for the bite-through moment. Stephanie pushed for shot two - the crisp exterior shattering - saying "Shot two's the one - if people are stopping their scroll, it's because they see that crisp exterior breaking apart, right?" Margaret agreed, prioritizing the crisp over the jalapeno cross-section Julian had championed. Shot two was locked in.

Thursday: Marcus brought finalized copy focusing on cornmeal's moisture-seal function. Margaret pushed to lead with the steam-absorption mechanic - not taste. Stephanie flagged repetition: "Love that you went all in on the moisture-seal thing - let's see what you've got?" Marcus tightened the structure to lead with cornmeal arresting moisture, then pivot to texture. Margaret approved - cut the explanation, show the fry result.

Friday: Margaret opened with "Final review. Shot two locked, copy locked. Someone tell me what isn't right, because something..." - expecting problems. Marcus pushed back, saying trusting the work improved it. Stephanie credited genuine listening over defending positions. Marcus and Julian agreed restraint drove both the copy and shot two - science-first writing and minimal angles made the difference.

Saturday: This conversation contains no discussion of "Jalapeno Corn Dog Bites" - it covers a website deployment. Devon confirmed verification passed and pushed the site live. Margaret advocated for restraint as a guiding principle, which Devon agreed with. No food ingredients or techniques were discussed.
